{"author": null, "closed_at": null, "collaborating_teams": null, "collaborating_users": null, "created_at": null, "credits": [{"login": "nvn1729", "type": "reporter"}], "credits_detailed": [{"state": "accepted", "type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/348774?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nvn1729/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nvn1729/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nvn1729/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nvn1729/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/nvn1729", "id": 348774, "login": "nvn1729", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjM0ODc3NA==", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nvn1729/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nvn1729/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nvn1729/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nvn1729/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/nvn1729/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/nvn1729", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-48818", "cvss": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": ["CWE-918"], "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-918", "name": "Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)"}], "description": "### Summary

When serving static files on Windows, `StaticFiles` resolves the requested path with [`os.path.realpath`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.path.html#os.path.realpath). If a UNC path (such as `\\attacker.com\share`) reaches the resolver, `realpath` causes the process to open a connection to the remote host over SMB (port 445). This is a server-side request forgery (SSRF) that leaks the service account's NTLMv2 credentials to the attacker-controlled host, which can then be cracked offline or relayed to other hosts.

### Details

`StaticFiles.lookup_path()` joins the requested path onto the served directory and calls [`os.path.realpath`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.path.html#os.path.realpath) on the result before checking containment with [`os.path.commonpath`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.path.html#os.path.commonpath). On Windows, a UNC path is absolute, so [`os.path.join`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.path.html#os.path.join) discards the served directory and `realpath` resolves the bare UNC path, triggering the outbound SMB connection and NTLM authentication before the containment check rejects the path. The HTTP response is a benign 404, but the credential disclosure has already happened. POSIX systems are not affected.

This only affects the default configuration (`follow_symlink=False`), which uses [`os.path.realpath`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.path.html#os.path.realpath). The `follow_symlink=True` branch uses [`os.path.abspath`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.path.html#os.path.abspath), which performs no I/O.

### Impact

Applications running on Windows that serve files with `StaticFiles` (directly, or via a framework built on Starlette such as FastAPI) in the default configuration are affected. `StaticFiles` is typically unauthenticated, so any client can trigger the SMB connection and leak the service account's NTLMv2 hash. A secondary impact is discovering internal hosts reachable over SMB by timing responses for valid versus invalid addresses.

### Mitigation

Applications not running on Windows are not affected. On Windows, serving static files through a dedicated web server (such as nginx or IIS) instead of `StaticFiles` avoids the issue. Blocking outbound SMB (port 445) from the application host prevents the credential disclosure even if a UNC path is resolved.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-wqp7-x3pw-xc5r", "html_url": "https://github.com/Kludex/starlette/security/advisories/GHSA-wqp7-x3pw-xc5r", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-wqp7-x3pw-xc5r"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-48818"}], "private_fork": null, "published_at": "2026-05-23T16:58:47Z", "publisher": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/7353520?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kludex/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kludex/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kludex/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kludex/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/Kludex", "id": 7353520, "login": "Kludex", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjczNTM1MjA=", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kludex/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kludex/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kludex/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kludex/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kludex/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/Kludex", "user_view_type": "public"}, "severity": "high", "state": "published", "submission": null, "summary": "SSRF and NTLM credential theft via UNC paths in StaticFiles on Windows", "updated_at": "2026-05-28T03:34:14Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/repos/Kludex/starlette/security-advisories/GHSA-wqp7-x3pw-xc5r", "vulnerabilities": [{"package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "starlette"}, "patched_versions": "1.1.0", "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 1.1.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: SSRF and NTLM credential theft via UNC paths in StaticFiles on Windows
